description: This chart plots the variation of anti-phase boundary (APB) fault energies (unit: mJ/m²) as a function of aluminium content (25% to 37 at.%) in Fe-Al alloys with different ordered structures. Three data series are presented: APB I energy in B2 ordered Fe-Al alloys (solid dots with solid line) increases linearly with increasing Al content, from ~27 mJ/m² at 27 at.% Al to ~109 mJ/m² at 36 at.% Al; APB I energy in DO₃ ordered Fe-Al alloys (solid squares with short dashed line) stays within 65-80 mJ/m² in the 25 to 29 at.% Al range; APB II energy in DO₃ ordered Fe-Al alloys (hollow triangles with long dashed line) decreases linearly with increasing Al content, from ~85 mJ/m² at 26 at.% Al to ~35 mJ/m² at 30 at.% Al. The data are cited from previous literature, and are used to interpret the dislocation configurations and work hardening behavior of Fe-Al alloys: low APB energies in DO₃ ordered low-Al Fe-Al alloys lead to dissociated superdislocations, while higher APB energies in B2 ordered high-Al alloys keep superdislocations closely coupled.
